title: Payback Period Rule: If a company has an initial investment of 50000 and...
summary: Calculate the payback period by dividing the initial investment by annual cash inflows.
prompt: If a company has an initial investment of $50,000 and cash inflows of $15,000 for the first three years, what is the payback period?
solution: The payback period is calculated by summing the cash inflows until the initial investment is recovered. Year 1: $15,000, Year 2: $15,000, Year 3: $15,000. Total after 3 years = $45,000. Therefore, it takes slightly more than 3 years to recover the investment.
